title: SpecrewTestResult
description: The ONE optional, framework-NEUTRAL result contract a downstream verification command MAY emit DURING a Specrew recorded run (FR-015 amended 2026-07-13, T018) so its test counts gain evidence standing. Specrew validates and records this verbatim WITHOUT knowing the framework (Pester, pytest, Jest, Vitest, dotnet test, Maven, Gradle, Go, Rust, or custom). When a structured result is REQUESTED but is missing, malformed, stale, or schema-invalid, the recorded run FAILS LOUDLY and never degrades to a richer pass claim. Specrew NEVER parses human-readable console output to infer counts, and caller-supplied counts are forbidden.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Exactly '1.0' for this schema; a future shape bumps this.

result.description: The framework-neutral overall outcome the downstream reports.

counts.description: OPTIONAL per-category counts. Absent means the downstream reported an outcome without a count breakdown; present counts are recorded verbatim. AUTHORITATIVE MAXIMUM (2026-07-14, review finding f1 run 20260714T193411985): each count is bounded by Int64.MaxValue (9223372036854775807) so a schema-valid count is always representable and serializable verbatim by the recorder - a count beyond it is a contract violation, not a recordable value.
